name: Local Available Cook Modes Cavity 2
description:
A bitmask representing the raw cook modes that are actually available
   in the cooking engine on the appliance.
   See also 0x5113.lua/0x5213.lua, which is remote available cook modes, representing the
   cook modes that are available to the app with additional bits for cook timer and probe
   availability.
comment: Local Available Cook Modes Cavity 2: Multi-field sensor, may need custom handling to extract individual values.

name: Lower Cavity Current Cycle Stage
description:
The user stage of the current cook including the stage number and a bitmap of conditions required to advance to the next stage. A bitmap of `0` indicates that the stage will automatically advance.

Note: this is the stage reflected to the UI/user, it is not indicitive of the actual "stage" of the cook engine.
comment: Lower Cavity Current Cycle Stage: Multi-field sensor, may need custom handling to extract individual values.

name: Lower Oven Advance Cook Stage
description: This signal is intended to be incremented by clients to indicate the user has met conditions to advance the stage of a user interactive precision cook.
comment: Lower Oven Advance Cook Stage: Single numeric writable value.

name: Recipe Status
description:
Indicates the status of a running recipe on the system.

Note: the control only stores this data, it does not do anything with it.
comment: Recipe Status: Multi-field sensor, may need custom handling to extract individual values.

name: Manual Enhanced Sabbath Schedule Selected
description: The user selected sabbath schedule when in the enhanced sabbath manual modes
comment: Manual Enhanced Sabbath Schedule Selected: Sabbath mode toggle.

name: Time Of Day Synced With UI
description: Indicates whether the Time of Day has been synced with the UI

data.name: Time Of Day Synced With UI

comment: Time Of Day Synced With UI: Boolean read-only binary sensor.

name: Advantium Remote Cook Mode Configuration
description:
Use this ERD to determine, which cook modes are available remotely, and which options they support.

Note
- The `Available` option determine whether a mode is shown to the user.
- For all options except `Available` an enabled means client can modify the related data.
- If `Convection on time` of the `Precision Cook Custom` is not set then the unit is 120v.

| Option   | Exceptions |
| ----------------- | -------------------------- |
| Remote Control | If disabled the user can not modify the cook mode |
| Upper heaters on time | If disabled do not show. |
| Lower heaters on time | If disabled do not show. |
| Microwave on time | If disabled do not show. |
| Convection on time | If disabled do not show. |

comment: Advantium Remote Cook Mode Configuration: Multi-field sensor, may need custom handling to extract individual values.

name: Advantium Cook Status
description:
Current Cook Status.
 - The `Remote Request ID` is the same as the originating cook request.
 - See 0x5402 for the rules regarding `Remote Cook Request IDs`
comment: Advantium Cook Status: Cook status, not user-facing.

name: Advantium Cook Request
description:
ERD used to request a cook from the client.
- The `Remote Cook Request ID` is an ID generated by the requesting client. The only stipulation on the ID is that it must be different from the previous.
- If a cooking element isn't needed for a cook then the client should send 0 for that element. Ex. A convection bake will set the cook temperature to 350 and microwave power, upper element, lower element, and convection element to 0.

Depends on
- The requested cook mode should follow the constraint defined in 0x5400
comment: Advantium Cook Request: Multi-field sensor, may need custom handling to extract individual values.

name: Advantium Cook Time Remaining
description: Remaining cook time.
comment: Advantium Cook Time Remaining: Timer/duration value.

name: Advantium Cook Time Adjustment
description:
Adjustment to the current cook time.
- Ex. +30s, -5s, etc.

Depends on
- The `Cook Action` of 0x5401 and 0x5402 should not be `Stop`.
- The `Cook time adjustable` of 0x5403 should be `Adjustable`.
- The cook time after adjustment should be between the minimum and max of the cook mode in either 0x5407, 0x5408, or 0x5409
comment: Advantium Cook Time Adjustment: Multi-field sensor, may need custom handling to extract individual values.

name: Advantium Menu Tree Parametric Hash
description:
SHA-256 Hash of the menu tree parametric data
- Should be requested before showing the device's precision cooking cycles
comment: Advantium Menu Tree Parametric Hash: Raw data sensor, interpretation unclear.

name: Advantium Menu Tree Selection
description:
Advantium's menu tree item selection for remote invocation.

Note
- Client needs to download the related menu tree
- The tree menu can be requested with `GET v1/file/sha256/{hash}`

Depends on
- 0x5405 the hash used to request the menu tree
comment: Advantium Menu Tree Selection: Single numeric writable value.

name: Advantium Allowable Oven Cook Time
description:
The minimum and maximum allowable cook time for oven cook modes.

Note
- User should not be able to set higher than the max value.
- The start button should be disabled until the min is reached.

name: Advantium Remote JSON Cook Request
description:
ERD used to request a cook from the client.
- The `Remote Cook Request ID` is an ID generated by the requesting client. The only stipulation on the ID is that it must be different from the previous.
- If a cooking element isn't needed for a cook then the client should send 0 for that element. Ex. A convection bake will set the cook temperature to 350 and microwave power, upper element, lower element, and convection element to 0.
- Client should parse the platform json and set the recipe id / choice selections / remote menu item selected if they want the UI to match the precision recipe.
- Setting remote menu item selected to false will make the UI treat it as a generic cook request

Depends on
- The requested cook mode should follow the constraint defined in 0x5400
- The correct json file being uploaded with a hash matchin 0x5405
comment: Advantium Remote JSON Cook Request: Multi-field sensor, may need custom handling to extract individual values.

comment: Cooktop Configuration: Multi-field enum sensor, may need custom handling.

name: Cooktop Status
description:
Provides the following cooktop status information:
- overall cooktop status.
- each individual burner status.
- each individual burner power level.
comment: Cooktop Status: Multi-field sensor, may need custom handling to extract individual values.

name: Cooktop Built-In Temperature Sensors
description: Temperatures of the burners for the cooktop in F*100. ERDs 0x5771-5777 indicates which sensors are in use. If a sensor is not present it will return 0x0000
comment: Cooktop Built-In Temperature Sensors: Temperature sensor, unit not explicitly specified.

name: Extended Cooktop Status
description:
Provides the following cooktop status information:
- overall cooktop status.
- each individual burner status.
- each individual burner power level.
- each style of burner.

description: This records cooktop burner termination reason.

name: Closed-Loop Cooking Device Remote Control Enabled
description: Specifies whether the device for the active closed-loop cooking session can be controlled remotely.
comment: Closed-Loop Cooking Device Remote Control Enabled: Boolean read-only binary sensor.

name: Closed-Loop Cooking Device Status
description: Bluetooth connection status of the active closed-loop cooking device.

values.0: Not paired and not currently connected

comment: Closed-Loop Cooking Device Status: Multi-field sensor, may need custom handling to extract individual values.

name: Closed-Loop Cooking Target Temperature
description: The closed-loop cooking session target temperature.
comment: Closed-Loop Cooking Target Temperature: Temperature sensor, unit not explicitly specified.

name: Closed-Loop Cooking Current Temperature
description: The current temperature measure by the active closed-loop cooking device.
comment: Closed-Loop Cooking Current Temperature: Temperature sensor, unit not explicitly specified.

name: Target Closed-Loop Cooking Time
description: The target closed-loop cooking cook time, set remotely.
comment: Target Closed-Loop Cooking Time: Single numeric writable value.

name: Start Closed-Loop Cooking Cook Timer
description: Start request to run the closed-loop cooking cook timer.
comment: Auto-suggested: Start Closed-Loop Cooking Cook Timer

name: Elapsed Closed-Loop Cooking Time
description: The time that the closed-loop cooking cycle has been running after preheating.
comment: Elapsed Closed-Loop Cooking Time: Single numeric read-only sensor.

name: Closed-Loop Cooking Target Time Reached
description: Specifies whether the target closed-loop cooking target time has been reached.
comment: Closed-Loop Cooking Target Time Reached: Boolean read-only binary sensor.

name: Closed-Loop Cooking Device Battery State
description: The battery state of the active closed-loop cooking device.
comment: Closed-Loop Cooking Device Battery State: Enum status.

name: Closed Loop Cooking Supported Devices
description: Bitfield of supported devices.

name: Closed-Loop Cooking Supported Mode
description: Specifies which closed-loop cooking mode is supported.

values.1: Single Closed-Loop Device on Single Burner
values.2: Single Closed-Loop Device on Any Burner
values.3: Multiple Closed-Loop Devices on Any Burner
values.4: Multiple Closed-Loop Devices, One Device Per Side

comment: Closed-Loop Cooking Supported Mode: Enum read-only sensor.

name: Left Front Closed-Loop Cooking Configuration
description:
The closed loop cooking configuration parameters for the left front burner.

Writing to this ERD is only allowed if:
- The ERD 0x5771 'Primary Devices Status' has one of the following values
  - 'Device selected' (2)
  - 'Device selected and in local closed-loop control (editable)' (4)
  - 'Device selected and in remote closed-loop control' (5),
- The 'Target Temperature' is between the 'Minimum Settable Temperature' and 'Maximum Settable Temperature' given by ERD 0x5771

Set PID terms to 0xFF's if not adjusting.
comment: Left Front Closed-Loop Cooking Configuration: Multi-field sensor, may need custom handling to extract individual values.

name: Left Rear Closed-Loop Cooking Configuration
description:
The closed loop cooking configuration parameters for the left rear burner.

  - 'Device selected and in remote closed-loop control' (5),
- The 'Target Temperature' is between the 'Minimum Settable Temperature' and 'Maximum Settable Temperature' given by ERD 0x5772

Set PID terms to 0xFF's if not adjusting.

Set PID terms to 0xFF's if not adjusting.

description: This allows a cooktop to assign a map for a Bluetooth peripheral device to a cooktop burner.

name: Left Front Closed Loop Cooking Device Index
description: The index of the BLE device tied to the CLC session on this burner. Used to index into the BLE ERD ranges (Device Status: 0x6200-0x6204, Battery Status: 0x6228-0x622C, Hestan Device Status: 0x623C-0x6240). Value undefined when CLC session inactive.
comment: Left Front Closed Loop Cooking Device Index: Single numeric read-only sensor.

name: Left Rear Closed Loop Cooking Device Index
description: The index of the BLE device tied to the CLC session on this burner. Used to index into the BLE ERD ranges (Device Status: 0x6200-0x6204, Battery Status: 0x6228-0x622C, Hestan Device Status: 0x623C-0x6240). Value undefined when CLC session inactive.
comment: Left Rear Closed Loop Cooking Device Index: Single numeric read-only sensor.
